concerns.text: T012 defines the deliverable as loading a local 'data/raw/mpea_raw.csv' (manual ingestion placeholder). This silently relaxes FR-001 ('System MUST download and parse the MPEA database') and violates Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility: 'External datasets MUST be fetched from the same canonical source on every run'). The task must include a mechanism to fetch/verify the canonical source or explicitly document the manual step as a non-automated exception in the spec, not just the task.

T031 implements the 80/20 split logic 'ONLY if N ≥ 80' but omits the mandatory 'ELSE' clause from FR-004 ('ELSE the system MUST flag a Data Scarcity Warning and halt the pipeline'). Relying on T015 for the halt logic creates a semantic gap where the training script (T031) could theoretically proceed if T015's check is bypassed or fails, violating the strict 'halt' requirement of FR-004.

T023 states the system will 'flag or exclude' entries with CALPHAD-derived yield strengths. FR-003.3 mandates 'exclude those entries OR use only raw composition features'. The task's 'flag' option without a defined exclusion or fallback mechanism (raw features) is a semantic weakening of the requirement, potentially allowing invalid data to enter the pipeline if only flagged.

T035 describes saving results to 'model_results.json' but omits the specific statistical method required by FR-006 ('95% confidence intervals... using the percentile method from 1000 bootstrap resamples'). While T033 mentions the method, T035's deliverable description fails to explicitly bind the output to this specific method, creating a traceability gap for the acceptance criterion.
